Salar brine: high-grade, proven — but still under-extracted.

The Lithium Triangle alone holds more than half of the world's known lithium reserves, locked in the brines beneath the salt flats. The challenge is not finding the lithium — it is recovering more of it with less waste, less water, and lower cost per tonne.

Conventional evaporation ponds are slow, weather-dependent, and recover only a fraction of the available lithium. M.L.E. replaces the rate-limiting evaporation step with a continuous membrane separation, operating day and night regardless of conditions.
The result is battery-grade purified lithium permeate — delivered faster, at lower OpEx, from the same brine resource you already hold.
Four measurable outcomes at the asset level.
~39% OpEx reduction per cubic meter of feed
Membrane separation eliminates the energy and time cost of multi-stage evaporation. No solar dependency, no seasonal downtime.
Over 19% capacity uplift on the same brine volume
Higher recovery rate from each cubic meter of brine processed means more Li tonnes from the same reserve base without additional exploration.
Clean effluent supports sustainable brine management and is compatible with regulatory re-injection requirements in major salar jurisdictions.
No disruption to existing precipitation lines
Modular skid deployment integrates in parallel with existing process infrastructure. No capital write-off on current equipment.

Why every other DLE technology fails at geothermal assets.

Geothermal energy plants draw water from deep underground reservoirs at high temperature. The lithium dissolved in that water is extracted alongside the energy — but to maintain the reservoir, operators must re-inject the water at temperatures typically exceeding 60°C.

Every conventional Direct Lithium Extraction technology operates below 40°C. In a geothermal context this creates an impossible requirement: cool the water to use DLE, then reheat it before re-injection. The energy cost destroys the economics.
M.L.E. was developed with this constraint in mind. The membrane operates at temperatures up to 70–80°C, returning water to the reservoir at the temperature required, without a separate heating step.
M.L.E. is the only DLE technology whose operating range overlaps with geothermal re-injection requirements.
Lithium from heat — a revenue stream no other technology can unlock.
Operates at up to 70–80°C — compatible with geothermal return requirements
The lithium-selective membrane maintains selectivity and throughput at the temperatures geothermal plants require. No cooling step. No reheating step.
Recovers lithium economically at concentrations down to 20 ppm
At high throughput volumes, low-concentration assets become economic. The Golan, Israel reference project validates extraction from 20 ppm groundwater at meaningful scale.
Adds a revenue stream to energy operations
Geothermal operators earn from electricity generation. M.L.E. adds a second output — purified lithium permeate — from the same brine stream, without changing plant operations.
Low-concentration groundwater assets that fall outside the window of conventional DLE are within reach when high-throughput M.L.E. processing is applied.
20 ppm groundwater. High-throughput operation. Validated meaningful lithium recovery from a low-concentration aqueous source under real-world conditions.

Why low-concentration groundwater has been left behind.

Groundwater lithium assets exist worldwide, from the Golan Heights to aquifers across the American Southwest and Central Asia. Many of these are well-characterized, accessible, and legally permitted. The problem is concentration: most sit below 200 ppm, outside the economic operating window of conventional DLE.

Conventional DLE products rely on high lithium concentration to generate sufficient output per unit of capital deployed. At 20–100 ppm, the math breaks down. The volume of water required to generate meaningful lithium output overwhelms the economics.

M.L.E. addresses this through a combination of high membrane throughput and low capital cost per processed volume. The Golan Heights, Israel project validated extraction at 20 ppm under real-world conditions, demonstrating that the asset class is viable at scale.
M.L.E. extends the viable concentration range to a threshold ten times lower than conventional DLE, opening groundwater assets that have never been economic before.
What M.L.E. makes possible at groundwater assets.
Viable recovery from 20 ppm lithium — validated in the field
The Golan Heights, Israel project extracted lithium meaningfully from 20 ppm groundwater at high throughput. This is a real-world data point, not a modeled projection.
No temperature requirements — operates at ambient conditions
Unlike geothermal, groundwater feeds are typically at ambient temperature. M.L.E. processes these without any heating or cooling step, keeping operating costs predictable.
Additive to existing water management operations
For municipal, agricultural, or industrial operators already managing groundwater, M.L.E. can be added to existing infrastructure as a revenue-generating step with no changes to primary operations.
Groundwater lithium rights are priced assuming conventional DLE constraints. Operations validated at 20 ppm fundamentally revalue the economics of assets previously considered marginal.
20 ppm groundwater. High-throughput operation at ambient temperature. Validated meaningful lithium recovery under real-world conditions — the lowest concentration deployment in the DLE industry.
Water utilities and municipal operators, agricultural water managers, junior lithium explorers with low-concentration aquifer permits, and national water authorities in lithium-bearing regions.
Modular skid installation on existing water infrastructure. No process changes upstream or downstream. Service priced per cubic meter processed.
One constraint that eliminates most competing technology.
The economics of conventional DLE are built around high-concentration feeds. Groundwater breaks the model at the concentration level alone — before any consideration of capital cost or throughput.

Conventional DLE products require approximately 200 ppm Li to be economic. Most groundwater assets sit at 20–100 ppm. At those concentrations, output per unit of capital falls below viability — the process cost exceeds the lithium revenue.

High membrane throughput at low cost per processed volume. Combined with lithium selectivity that works at low concentrations, this shifts the break-even point down to 20 ppm — validated operationally, not theoretically.

The 20 percent you are currently throwing away.

Hard rock spodumene refiners lose approximately 20% of all available lithium to process wastewater — and industry-wide, only about 4% of that wastewater lithium is currently captured. M.L.E. bolts on to existing wastewater handling and converts the remainder from an environmental liability into a revenue line.

ppm Li in spodumene refinery wastewater

Upstream process changes required to deploy M.L.E.
A waste stream that the industry largely ignores.

Spodumene refiners — the processors who convert hard rock lithium ore into battery-grade material — generate large volumes of lithium-rich wastewater as a byproduct of the refining process. At concentrations exceeding 30,000 ppm, this stream carries enormous lithium value.

Despite this, the industry captures an estimated 4% of that lithium. The remainder is discharged as tailings — a financial loss and, in many jurisdictions, a growing environmental liability as discharge standards tighten.

M.L.E. is a bolt-on solution. It integrates with the wastewater handling infrastructure already in place, requires no modification to upstream refining lines, and converts the discharge stream into a revenue-generating output.

Every barrel of produced water already contains lithium. None of it is being recovered.

Produced water is the water that comes to the surface alongside oil and gas during extraction. It is a byproduct that operators in the US Gulf states and other producing regions spend heavily to manage — injecting it underground, treating it, or disposing of it through licensed facilities.
The same produced water commonly carries 100 to 500 ppm of dissolved lithium — a concentration range that M.L.E. can recover economically, particularly at high throughput volumes.

No other extraction technology changes the economic calculus of produced water faster. M.L.E. drops a modular skid into existing water handling infrastructure, begins recovering lithium, and leaves hydrocarbon operations entirely unchanged.

Produced water from oil & gas operations can contain 20-500 mg/L lithium. With over 3 billion barrels of produced water generated annually in the U.S. alone, the recoverable lithium value represents billions of dollars in untapped revenue for operators.
Produced water economics — before and after M.L.E.

Direct Cathode Manufacture is our step-change innovation. It converts purified lithium directly into battery-ready cathode powder on site, with no intermediate salts, no refinery, and no cathode chemistry lock-in. It multiplies the value of every lithium atom recovered.

D.C.M. pairs with M.L.E. for a complete brine-to-battery platform, but it stands on its own. It bolts onto any purified lithium stream, so operators who already extract lithium can vertically integrate without rebuilding upstream. Certified cathode sells at roughly $655k per ton equivalent, against about $28k for raw Li₂CO₃.
Where D.C.M. deploys, independent of M.L.E.

Already producing purified lithium with your own DLE or evaporation process? D.C.M. plugs into that stream and turns carbonate-grade output into battery-ready cathode, capturing the refining margin in-house.
Refiners producing lithium hydroxide or carbonate can add a cathode line on site, moving up the value chain instead of shipping salts to a downstream cathode maker.
Producers selling into a compressed refining market can convert that same output into a certified cathode product and sell directly to cell manufacturers worldwide.
OEMs committed to domestic battery supply can secure cathode-grade output close to home, without building or contracting a centralized refinery.
NMC, LFP, LMFP, NCA, and emerging chemistries. The client or open market dictates the cathode spec.
Go from lithium to battery-ready cathode on site, capturing value that conventional chains spread across many intermediaries.
Certified cathode material sells directly to battery manufacturers worldwide, no centralized refiner required.

Multiply the value of every Li atom.

The saleable value of lithium multiplies exponentially through the conventional supply chain. Conventional operation limits the revenues of Li asset owners to low-value intermediary salts. D.C.M. empowers proprietors to access the full price point with minimal relative cost.
Li asset owners typically exit at Li₂CO₃ or LiOH, capturing only the first $28k–$75k per ton equivalent.
$655k per ton equivalent. D.C.M. empowers Li asset owners to access the full price point.
The supply chain leaves money on the table.

Today, lithium asset operators sell carbonate or hydroxide into a refining market they do not control. Refiners convert that into cathode precursor. Cathode manufacturers convert that into cathode powder. The margin compounds at every step. Asset operators see none of it.
D.C.M. collapses that chain. Lithium goes from purified permeate to battery-ready cathode powder in a single integrated process, on site.

Purified lithium from any aqueous feed is concentrated onto a proprietary solid-phase capture material. This material is the bridge between the aqueous separation step and the solid cathode product. Note: M.L.E. and D.C.M. can be deployed independently or together — the feed source is not a determining factor. Both technologies are agnostic to chemistry and feed type.

The captured lithium is transferred directly onto cathode precursor material. The output is a battery-ready cathode powder, chemistry-matched to the customer's specification. There is no intermediate salt, no transport to a refiner, no conversion plant.

D.C.M. lets operators sell into the cathode market directly, capture refining margin in-house, and avoid building or contracting a refinery. It is agnostic to cathode chemistry, which means the same platform can output LFP, NMC, or future chemistries as the market shifts.
capture multiple stages of the supply chain in a single integrated facility
M.L.E. and D.C.M. are both agnostic to feed chemistry — switch cathode chemistries without rebuilding, and deploy on any aqueous lithium source
reduce dependency on geographically concentrated refining capacity
D.C.M. is not locked to a single cathode chemistry. NMC, LFP, LMFP, NCA, and emerging chemistries can all be produced. The client or open market dictates the cathode spec.
Proprietors of Li assets can go directly from brine to battery-ready cathode on-site. D.C.M. captures the value that conventional supply chains distribute across multiple intermediaries.

Uniform crystal structure, monodisperse particle distribution, and optional co-manufacture of graphene coating, producing cathode material that meets and exceeds market specifications.
D.C.M. is designed for on-site, autonomous operation. Minimal staffing requirements, modular design, and remote monitoring allow deployment in remote or resource-constrained locations.
D.C.M. integrates directly with existing mines, DLE systems, or M.L.E. output. No need to reconfigure upstream operations. D.C.M. plugs into the purified brine stream.
A category with no direct competition.

There is no known direct competitor in continuous brine-to-cathode production. The conventional supply chain requires 4–6 separate industrial steps, each performed by a different company in a different geography.
D.C.M. collapses this chain, allowing Li asset owners to control output quality, timing, and chemistry specification independently of external refining capacity.

greenLi | Lithium extraction from any water source
Monetize lithium assets across the full value chain.

) deploy independently or in tandem to extract aqueous lithium and manufacture lithium-ion battery cathode materials on-site. Our 'brine-to-battery' system installs as a containerized, bolt-on solution, letting operators capture margin across the full supply chain.

The lithium supply chain faces structural constraints that limit the industry's ability to meet growing demand. Conventional lithium extraction methods recover just 25-50% of available lithium in a given source. The inefficacy of current methodologies is compounded by the associated costs and time: salar evaporation cycles stretch 18 months; hard-rock refining loses significant lithium capacity to process wastewater; and geographically isolated refining infrastructure causes supply chain bottlenecks, with EV & battery manufacturers absorbing those costs.
Demand is rapidly outpacing supply, but the solution is not finding new sources of lithium; it is enabling the ability to
recover previously inaccessible lithium from existing resources
. Without an efficient and sustainable brine-to-battery solution, the EV revolution will not sustain its growth.
From your water source to a battery.
Purifies any aqueous Li source into battery-grade permeate.
Generates battery-ready cathode materials directly from purified Li brines.

One membrane platform runs on salar brine, produced water, geothermal fluid, refinery wastewater, and low-concentration groundwater. If an aqueous source carries lithium, greenLi recovers it, at any concentration.

DCM builds battery-ready cathode materials, agnostic with any battery chemistry desired by manufacturers or consumers. As battery roadmaps shift, greenLi can shift with it, keeping our output aligned with market demand.
Containerized skids bolt on to existing operation infrastructure, deploying modularly using COTS parts. Extraction and cathode manufacture run on site, capturing margin across the full value chain.
MLE: Transposing RO Desalination onto Lithium Extraction
Brine-agnostic. A continuous membrane platform that brings the proven economics of reverse osmosis desalination to lithium extraction, deployable on any aqueous source, at any concentration.

Unlike batch-process DLE technologies that cycle through adsorption and desorption phases, M.L.E. runs 24/7. No downtime for regeneration, no 18-month evaporation cycles. Just continuous throughput from feed to purified brine.

The platform ships in containerized skids that bolt onto existing infrastructure. Deploy at a salar, an oilfield, a geothermal facility, or a hard-rock refinery. No new factory required, no site-specific engineering from scratch.

The Li-selective membrane manufactures through established desalination supply chains. Scale-up does not depend on proprietary manufacturing. It leverages existing global capacity. Cost and lead times follow a well-understood learning curve.
DCM: From raw materials to battery-ready cathode, on-site, on demand

Most lithium asset operators stop at carbonate or hydroxide, selling into a refining market where margins compress and dependencies sit outside their control. Traditional battery manufacture builds cathode structures around lithium as an input from centralized refiners.

Direct Cathode Manufacture (D.C.M.) is different. It converts purified lithium directly into battery-ready cathode powder on site, with no intermediate salts, no transport to a refiner, and no cathode chemistry lock-in. Operators capture the margin that today flows to cell manufacturers.

greenLi's platform deploys directly on salar operations and brine prospecting permits, recovering substantially more lithium from the same reserve volume. Operators gain more output from existing assets without expanding their footprint.

For spodumene refiners, M.L.E. captures the 20 percent of lithium currently lost to process wastewater. A new revenue stream recovered from what was previously a disposal cost, with no disruption to existing operations.

Oil and gas operators producing water from lithium-bearing formations can capture lithium as a co-product rather than paying for disposal. A meaningful new revenue stream added to an existing operation, without changes to the core business.

Geothermal plants and low-concentration groundwater assets can unlock lithium recovery for the first time. M.L.E. is the only extraction technology compatible with 60-degree Celsius return water and sub-50 ppm concentrations.

Separate lithium from competing ions at any concentration, from 0.17 ppm seawater to 30,000 ppm refinery brine. Continuous operation. No evaporation. No batch chemistry.
Compared to conventional salar brine processing.
Higher recovery on the same feed

Membrane Lithium Extraction (M.L.E.) captures the Li that conventional evaporation and purification methods leave behind, lifting recovery from typical 25–50% baselines to over 80%, generating more product for the same asset.

Continuous, RO-style cascade replaces multi-step purification trains, reducing reagent use, footprint, and labour. Modular skids can be deployed rapidly, allowing flexible ramp-up for dynamic conditions.

By employing greenLi's patent-pending Li-selective membrane, M.L.E. purifies brines without the precipitants of conventional processing, the acidic eluants of conventional Direct Lithium Extraction (D.L.E.), or any associated transport costs.
Figures based on internal modelling and bench-scale validation against salar brine processing baselines.
Agnostic to the aqueous source of Li.
When combined with the right pre-treatment, M.L.E. operates across the full salinity spectrum, from ultra-dilute seawater to concentrated refinery wastewater. The same modular hardware, every feed.

M.L.E. operates as a continuous flow process. Feed water enters the system, passes through stacked membrane modules, and exits with lithium concentrated on the permeate side. Competing ions (sodium, magnesium, calcium, boron) are rejected.

The membrane is developed at Tel Aviv University and manufactures through established desalination supply chains, which means scale-up does not depend on a new factory. Separate lithium from competing ions at any concentration, from 0.17 ppm seawater to 30,000 ppm refinery brine.

Conventional D.L.E. works on a narrow band of feed conditions. Concentration above roughly 200 ppm. Temperatures below 40 degrees Celsius. Stable chemistry. Anything outside that band fails or operates at uneconomic recovery.
M.L.E. is different. It is bound by throughput, not by concentration. That has three consequences:
Low-concentration assets become viable. Groundwater at 20 ppm produces meaningful lithium output when flow is high enough.
High-temperature streams become viable. M.L.E. operates up to 70 to 80 degrees Celsius, which is the only way to handle geothermal return water at 60 degrees Celsius.

High-volume assets gain capacity. By recovering over 80 percent of available lithium, versus the 25 to 50 percent typical of evaporation, and running continuously rather than through an 18-month cycle, the same brine resource yields meaningfully more lithium per year.

Most D.L.E. methods rely on ion-exchange resins, which run in batch cycles and depend on chemical regenerants. M.L.E. replaces that approach with a continuous membrane platform that outperforms across the criteria that matter for commercial deployment.

Membrane Lithium Extraction (M.L.E.) purifies the raw lithium asset. Direct Cathode Manufacture (D.C.M.) produces the final product. Together they let asset operators capture margin across the entire supply chain.

The saleable value of a lithium atom multiplies by more than 100 times as it moves from raw brine to battery cathode. Asset operators today capture the bottom of that curve. greenLi's platform lets them capture more of it.
Purifies any aqueous Li source into high value permeate.

Using a proprietary Li-selective membrane developed at Tel Aviv University, our M.L.E. system separates Li from competing ions, purifying Li-containing aqueous resources at any concentration. Our system transposes the desalination industry into an effective, continuous Direct Lithium Extraction (D.L.E.) solution.

With a revolutionary approach, our proprietary system offers a "brine-to-battery" solution that generates high quality battery-ready cathode powders directly from purified Li brines. With D.C.M., proprietors of lithium assets can vertically integrate down the supply chain.
